Rewrite line 224 `geas task record add --task {id} --section gate_result --file <gate_result_json_file>` → pipe or redirection form (`geas task record add --task {id} --section gate_result < <gate_result_json_file>`). (or use --file with a JSON file)` — rewrite the parenthetical to describe the stdin heredoc form instead. Rewrite line 69 `geas task record add --task {id} --section implementation_contract --file <contract_json_file>` → `geas task record add --task {id} --section implementation_contract < <contract_json_file>` (or heredoc variant). Step 14 — plugin/skills/memorizing/SKILL.md. No actionable JSON --data hits. All three matches (lines 77, 130, 144) are `geas event log --type ... --data '{...}'` — the allowed exception. Leave untouched.

Step 15 — plugin/agents/authority/challenger.md. Rewrite the multiline block at lines 89-92: `geas task record add --task {task_id} --section challenge_review \\\n  --data '{"concerns":[...],"blocking":false,"summary":"..."}'` → heredoc form `geas task record add --task {task_id} --section challenge_review <<'EOF'\n{"concerns":[...],"blocking":false,"summary":"..."}\nEOF`. Preserve the indentation inside the fenced code block.

Step 16 — plugin/agents/software/qa-engineer.md. Line 99 contains the prose hint `--set "criteria_results=<use --file for complex results>"`. This is not a geas-CLI-JSON-input invocation (it's a --set value), but the `<use --file ...>` language refers to the removed --file flag. Rewrite the hint to reference stdin/heredoc or external-file redirection terminology so agents do not attempt --file. No JSON command rewriting here.

Step 17 — Final grep verification. Run `grep -rnE "geas [a-z-]+ .*--data '" plugin/skills/ plugin/agents/` and confirm every remaining hit is one of the allowed exceptions: `geas event log --data`, `geas context write --data`. Run `grep -rnE "geas [a-z-]+ .*--file " plugin/skills/ plugin/agents/` and confirm every remaining hit is `geas packet create --file`. Zero other hits expected. Report match counts in the implementation evidence.
edge_cases: Multi-line JSON payloads inside heredoc: use `<<'EOF' ... EOF` (single-quoted delimiter) to prevent shell expansion of `$`, backticks, and `!`. This is mandated by acceptance criterion 6.

Embedded single quotes inside JSON: heredoc form removes the single-quote escaping problem entirely since no outer `'...'` wrapper is needed. Prefer heredoc over here-string `<<<'...'` when JSON contains apostrophes (e.g. in summary text).
Short single-line JSON: prefer here-string `<<<'{...}'` for brevity, fall back to heredoc when JSON contains single quotes.

Payloads containing secret-looking strings (tokens, passwords): do NOT include such values in documentation examples; use placeholder tokens like `<token>` or `***REDACTED***` and ensure heredoc delimiter quoting is `<<'EOF'` so any `$VAR` inside is not expanded.

Fenced markdown code blocks with indentation: when the original command is inside an indented code block (e.g. list item with 3-space indent), the replacement heredoc must preserve the same leading indent on every line of the heredoc body AND the closing `EOF` delimiter must be at column 0 of the heredoc context OR match the indentation rules of `<<-EOF` — prefer keeping the whole heredoc on the same indent level as the opening command and use a single-line `<<<` form when possible to avoid indentation ambiguity.

Bash("...") wrapper examples: the command is inside a double-quoted Bash tool argument. Inside Bash("..."), newlines inside heredocs are problematic for the documentation reader. For these inline wrappers, prefer the single-line here-string form `Bash("geas ... <<<'{...}'")` with escaped inner double quotes (`\"`). When the JSON is too long for one line, switch the example to a fenced bash block outside the Bash() wrapper.

Agent() prompt strings embedding geas commands: these are prose instructions to sub-agents. Rewrite the embedded command text to the heredoc/stdin form as the canonical example the sub-agent should copy, not as a literal shell line the orchestrator itself will execute.

Allowed exception preservation: `geas event log --data '...'`, `geas context write --data '...'`, `geas packet create --file ...`, `geas packet create --content '...'` MUST NOT be rewritten. The final grep step treats these as expected residuals.

qa-engineer.md line 99 edge case: the `<use --file for complex results>` string is a human-readable hint inside a --set value, not a flag invocation. It still needs updating so agents do not follow stale guidance — this is a prose/documentation accuracy fix, not a command-form rewrite.
implementation_contract.state_transitions: Document-only edits. No .geas/ runtime state changes, no CLI source changes, no schema changes, no agent routing changes, no task-state transitions triggered by these edits. Semantic meaning of every example must remain identical — only the command invocation form changes from `--data '<json>'` / `--file <path>` to stdin heredoc / here-string / `<` redirection.
non_goals: Do not rebundle plugin/bin/geas (reserved for task-003).
Do not modify any CLI source under src/cli/ (already completed in task-001).
Do not translate or sync changes to docs/ko/ (out of scope — separate mission).
Do not touch the allowed value-flag exceptions: `geas event log --data`, `geas context write --data`, `geas packet create --file`, `geas packet create --content`.
Do not edit any file under docs/protocol/ — the protocol docs are not in the scope surface list.
Do not modify .geas/rules.md or any memory artifacts.
Do not alter JSON payload content or field names in examples — only the invocation form changes.
Do not introduce new examples, new flags, or new skills — edits are restricted to rewriting existing invocations.
demo_steps: AC1 (mission/references/*.md --data/--file JSON converted): `grep -nE "geas [a-z-]+ .*--data '|geas [a-z-]+ .*--file " plugin/skills/mission/references/` → every remaining hit must be `geas event log --data` (the allowed exception). Expected zero non-exception hits across specifying/polishing/pipeline/orchestration/building/evolving.

AC2 (7 SKILL.md files converted): `grep -nE "geas [a-z-]+ .*--data '|geas [a-z-]+ .*--file " plugin/skills/vote-round/SKILL.md plugin/skills/verify-fix-loop/SKILL.md plugin/skills/task-compiler/SKILL.md plugin/skills/evidence-gate/SKILL.md plugin/skills/intake/SKILL.md plugin/skills/implementation-contract/SKILL.md plugin/skills/memorizing/SKILL.md` → every remaining hit must be `geas event log --data` or `geas context write --data`.

AC3 (2 agent files converted): `grep -nE "geas [a-z-]+ .*--data '|geas [a-z-]+ .*--file " plugin/agents/authority/challenger.md plugin/agents/software/qa-engineer.md` → zero hits (challenger had 1 multiline `task record add --data` which becomes heredoc; qa-engineer had only a prose hint).

AC4 (JSON semantics preserved): diff each edit manually — every heredoc body must contain the byte-identical JSON that was previously inside the `--data '...'` quotes (same field order, same placeholders). Run `git diff plugin/skills/ plugin/agents/` and inspect for any JSON content drift.

AC5 (value-flag exceptions untouched): `grep -nE "geas event log .*--data|geas context write .*--data|geas packet create .*--(file|content)" plugin/skills/ plugin/agents/` → count before edits == count after edits. Record both counts in evidence.

AC6 (single-quoted heredoc delimiter): `grep -nE "<<[^']*EOF" plugin/skills/ plugin/agents/` → zero unquoted `<<EOF` hits in newly added heredocs. Every heredoc introduced by this task uses `<<'EOF'` (or equivalent single-quoted delimiter).

AC7 (task-003 verifiable zero state): final repo-wide verification command `grep -rnE "geas [a-z-]+ .*--data '" plugin/skills/ plugin/agents/ | grep -vE "event log|context write"` returns zero lines, and `grep -rnE "geas [a-z-]+ .*--file " plugin/skills/ plugin/agents/ | grep -vE "packet create"` returns zero lines. These two commands form the task-003 acceptance bar.

known_risks: Grep pattern false negatives: regex --data '|--file  assumes spacing; multiline string embeddings or unusual quoting could evade the scan
Docs now describe stdin patterns but the bundled plugin/bin/geas is stale — readers may copy examples that do not yet run against the shipped binary until task-003 rebundles

Rendering of heredoc EOF inside nested Agent() prompt strings — visually verified only, not machine-rendered

Non-goals preserved: event log --data, context write --data, packet create --file/--content untouched (23 allowed residuals, all event log). Grep verify clean. Contract ACs 7/7.

closure.change_summary: plugin/skills와 plugin/agents 문서에서 20개의 JSON-input --data/--file 사용 예시를 stdin pipe/heredoc 패턴으로 전수 치환 (13개 파일). <<'EOF' 형태로 셸 확장 방지, 펜스드 코드 블록 들여쓰기 보존. 비-JSON 값 플래그(event log --data, context write --data, packet create --file/--content)는 보존.

7/7 ACs; grep finds 0 violations, 23 allowed event log residuals

open_risks: docs describe stdin patterns but bundled plugin/bin/geas is stale until task-003 rebundles

verdict.rationale: Closure packet complete. 7/7 ACs verified via grep, gate pass across all 4 rubric dimensions (core 4/4, feature 5/4, quality 4/4, regression 5/5), three independent reviewers (technical-writer, design-authority, qa-engineer) approved. 20 JSON-input invocations rewritten to stdin heredoc across 13 files with byte-identical JSON preserved; allowed exceptions (event log/context write/packet create) untouched. Scope faithful to contract, non-goals honored (no bin rebundle, no src/cli edits, no docs/ko sync). Known risk of stale plugin/bin/geas is explicitly carried to task-003 which will rebundle. Product fit: removes stale --data/--file guidance so agents stop copying broken examples.

what_went_well: Scope enumeration (20 actionable sites across 13 files) was precomputed in impl contract, eliminating mid-implementation surprises
Heredoc choice rule (<<'EOF' quoted to prevent shell expansion) applied consistently across all multiline payloads
grep-based acceptance criteria gave the gate a mechanical, unambiguous pass signal
Non-goal preservation (event log / context write / packet create) cleanly respected — 23 allowed residuals all event log
what_broke: nothing blocking; self-check flagged advisory debt only (stale bundle until task-003)
what_was_surprising: plugin/skills/vote-round and plugin/skills/memorizing had zero JSON-input matches despite being in scope — pure event log usage
plugin/skills/evidence-gate/SKILL.md had a `--file` case that converts naturally to `<` redirection form
rule_candidates: Docs sweep tasks should pre-enumerate hit counts per file during implementation contract to set scope expectations accurately
memory_candidates: technical-writer: For CLI docs sweeps, prefer <<<'single-line' for simple JSON and <<'EOF' heredoc for multiline; use < path for file-backed inputs
next_time_guidance: During impl contract, precompute per-file match counts via grep to catch contract-scope misalignments early
